changes_made: Changed k8s_resource_requests_and_limits_set to k8s_pod_resource_requests_and_limits_set for clarity and consistency with resource naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are relevant to Kubernetes capacity management. They map well to the SOC2 control A1.1 by addressing autoscaling and resource allocation. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor adjustment was made to align with the pattern of specifying the resource type.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource type is specified in function names for clarity, such as using 'pod' for resource requests and limits.
title: A1.1 Capacity Management: The entity conducts capacity planning to ensure adequate system capacity
step1_reasoning: The SOC2 control A1.1 focuses on capacity planning to ensure adequate system capacity. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that the cluster can scale appropriately and that workloads have defined resource requests and limits to manage capacity effectively. The Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler and Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) are directly related to capacity management. Additionally, setting resource requests and limits on pods ensures that workloads are scheduled based on available capacity. Monitoring node resource utilization helps in proactive capacity planning.
